Unit 3: NumPy — Numerical Computing Foundation
Chapter 8: Introduction to NumPy
Why NumPy? The Performance Gap Between Python Lists and NumPy Arrays

Exercise: Create arrays representing monthly rainfall data (in mm) for 5 Indian cities over 12 months. Reshape into a 5×12 matrix. Extract monsoon months (June–September) using slicing. Identify the city-month with highest rainfall using indexing.
Creating Arrays: np.array(), np.zeros(), np.ones(), np.arange(), np.linspace(), Reshaping Arrays: reshape(), flatten(), and ravel()

Element-Wise Operations: Arithmetic on Arrays Without Loops

Exercise: Using the rainfall dataset — compute average rainfall per city (axis=1), average per month (axis=0), identify months where rainfall exceeded 200mm (boolean indexing), and generate 1000 simulated temperature readings using np.random.normal(). Compare simulated mean and standard deviation with input parameters.
Broadcasting: How NumPy Handles Operations on Different-Shaped Arrays
